package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for busy_waiting: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: select { default: ... } inside loops, which often spins instead of blocking.
type CaseBusyWaitingDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseBusyWaitingModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseBusyWaitingRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseBusyWaitingModel
}

type CaseBusyWaitingService struct {
    repo *CaseBusyWaitingRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseBusyWaitingAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveBusyWaiting(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseBusyWaitingDTO) error {
    focus := "busy_waiting"
    _ = focus
    for i := 0; i < 2; i++ { time.Sleep(time.Millisecond) }
    busy_waiting_positive := map[string]int{"busy": 1, "waiting": 2}
    busy_waiting_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    busy_positive_0 := busy_waiting_positive["busy"] + len(input)
    _ = busy_positive_0
    waiting_positive_1 := busy_waiting_positive["waiting"] + len(input)
    _ = waiting_positive_1
    return nil
}
